question: Who is the petitioner in this case?
ground_truth: Sanjay Kumar

question: What is the father's name of Sanjay Kumar?
ground_truth: Naresh Prasad

question: What is the case number of this writ petition?
ground_truth: Civil Writ Jurisdiction Case No. 2413 of 2020

question: In which High Court was this case heard?
ground_truth: High Court of Judicature at Patna

question: On what date was the judgment delivered?
What was the registration number of the motorcycle sought to be released?
Which police station case was connected to the seized motorcycle?
ground_truth: Kauakol (Rupau) P.S. Case No. 59/18

question: On what date was Kauakol (Rupau) P.S. Case No. 59/18 registered?
Under which section of the Bihar Prohibition and Excise Act was the police case registered?
ground_truth: Section 30(a)

question: In which court was the related criminal case pending?
ground_truth: Court of Special Judge Excise Act, Nawada

question: What relief did the petitioner seek in this writ petition?
ground_truth: Release of the Passion Pro motorcycle bearing registration number BR-21-H-0998

question: Which state is involved in this case?
ground_truth: Bihar

question: Who was the first respondent in this case?
ground_truth: The State of Bihar through Principal Secretary, Department of Excise and Prohibition, Government of Bihar, Patna

question: Who represented the petitioner?
ground_truth: Mr. Subodh Kumar, Mr. Mayank Kumar, and Mr. Priya Ranjan Kumar

question: Who represented the respondents?
ground_truth: Mr. Vikash Kumar (SC-11)

question: Which Act is discussed extensively in the judgment?
ground_truth: The Bihar Prohibition and Excise Act, 2016

question: According to the judgment, what activities does Section 13 of the Bihar Prohibition and Excise Act prohibit unless otherwise allowed?
ground_truth: Manufacture, storage, distribution, transportation, possession, sale, purchase, and consumption of intoxicants or liquor

question: Which section of the Act lays down the procedure for confiscation of things used in the commission of an offence?
ground_truth: Section 56

question: Under Section 56, are vehicles used for carrying intoxicants liable for confiscation?
ground_truth: Yes, vehicles used for carrying intoxicants are liable for confiscation.

question: Under which section does the power to issue an order of confiscation vest with the District Collector or Authorized Officer?
ground_truth: Section 58

question: Who has the authority to issue an order of confiscation under the Act?
ground_truth: The District Collector or Authorized Officer

question: What remedy is available against a confiscation order under the Act?
ground_truth: Appeal under Section 92 and Revision under Section 93

question: Which previous case did the petitioner rely upon for disposal of the petition?
ground_truth: Md. Shaukat Ali vs. The State of Bihar (CWJC No. 20598 of 2019)

question: Which other case was cited along with Md. Shaukat Ali?
ground_truth: Umesh Sah versus the State of Bihar & Ors. (CWJC No. 17165 of 2019)

question: Which case decided on 29.01.2020 was also relied upon by the petitioner?
ground_truth: Bunilal Sah @ Munilal Sah (CWJC No. 2050 of 2020)

question: What issue did the Court observe regarding confiscation proceedings?
ground_truth: Many cases involved non-initiation of confiscation proceedings or illegal confiscation orders.

question: According to the Court, what was one reason for the large number of petitions being filed?
ground_truth: Failure to initiate confiscation proceedings under Section 58.

question: According to the Court, what happens after the seizing officer submits a report regarding seized property?
ground_truth: The District Collector or Authorized Officer is required to pass an order regarding confiscation.

question: Under Section 56(e), what kind of premises may be confiscated?
ground_truth: Premises used for storing or manufacturing liquor or intoxicants or for committing any offence under the Act.

question: According to the explanation to Section 56, what does the term 'premises' include?
ground_truth: The immovable structure, all movable items within the structure, and the land on which the premises is situated.
